key_insight: The paper derives a computable a posteriori error bound for deep neural FBSDE solvers that explicitly accounts for control mismatch between the forward auxiliary control and the backward network output.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical diagnostic tool to assess consistency and reproducibility of decoupled deep-learning solvers for fully coupled stochastic control problems.
method: Proves a continuous-time stability estimate under perturbations in drift, diffusion, generator, terminal condition, and auxiliary control.
Transfers the stability result to a discrete-time setting to obtain computable indicators.
Uses three error terms: terminal defect, pathwise residual, and control mismatch; the mismatch term disappears when controls coincide.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests on a linear-quadratic FBSDE with an explicit reference solution and a multidimensional Burgers-type FBSDE without a reference solution.
watch_item: The bound relies on an idealized discrete adapted trajectory and on accurately estimating the mismatch term, which may be sensitive in practical neural implementations.
paper_title: A Posteriori Error Analysis for Decoupled Neural Approximations of Fully Coupled FBSDEs with Control Mismatch
